creator: msl0000023508@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to David Chisnall from comment #9)

Some machines assign the serial port to a non-default x86 I/O port. I suggest check whether your machine is the case. On a booted system, you can use a shell command like 'ls -l /dev/ttyu*' to list probed serial consoles.

For example on (most?) Dell PowerEdge systems, the virtual serial port is ttyu0, and the physical serial port is ttyu1.
